skill.name: nature-citation
skill.description: Add strict Nature/CNS citations to manuscript text by splitting long passages into citable segments, searching only accepted flagship and subjournal titles from Nature Portfolio, the AAAS Science family, and Cell Press, filtering by publication time range, and exporting one reference-manager-ready output by default.
skill.summary: Finds Nature/CNS series citations for manuscript text and exports EndNote/RIS/Zotero-ready files.
skill.author: Yuan1z skill, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 308 static findings. The vast majority are false positives: 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are Markdown code formatting (not actual execution), 'weak crypto' flags match 'md5' in documentation contexts, 'certificate/key' flags refer to citation keys, and 'system reconnaissance' flags are CSS class names. The script makes legitimate HTTP requests to api.crossref.org for citation metadata search and writes output files locally. One low-severity XSS risk exists in the self-contained HTML browser output where template literals render Crossref API data via innerHTML.

medium_findings.title: XSS risk via innerHTML with API-sourced data in self-contained HTML output
medium_findings.description: The generated HTML browser template at scripts/nature_citation.py:1831 uses wrapper.innerHTML with template literals rendering item.title, item.journal, and item.year from Crossref API responses. If Crossref metadata contained malicious HTML/script content in title or journal fields, it would execute when the local HTML file is opened. Risk is limited because the output is a self-contained local file for review purposes, not a web application. Crossref data is generally trusted scholarly metadata.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed innerHTML usage with unsanitized template interpolation of API data, but output is a local HTML review artifact, not a deployed web application. Crossref is a trusted academic metadata source.

low_findings.title: External HTTP requests to Crossref API
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to api.crossref.org/works for citation metadata search. This is expected functionality for a citation tool. No data exfiltration or suspicious endpoints detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Crossref is the standard scholarly metadata API; requests use polite pool mailto parameter and configurable retry logic. No credential theft or exfiltration patterns present.

low_findings.title: Local filesystem output writes
low_findings.description: Script writes citation export files (.enw, .ris, .rdf) and review artifacts to user-specified or current directory. Expected behavior for a file-export tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Uses standard pathlib operations to write reference manager files. No path traversal or sensitive directory access detected.

content.user_title: Add Nature/CNS Citations to Your Manuscript
content.value_statement: Academic writers struggle to find high-impact Nature, Science, and Cell citations to support claims in their manuscripts. This skill splits manuscript text into segments and searches only accepted flagship and subjournal titles to produce reference-manager-ready citation files.
actual_capabilities: Splits manuscript paragraphs into citable segments using configurable character limits
Searches Crossref metadata API restricted to Nature Portfolio, AAAS Science family, and Cell Press journals
Filters candidate papers by publication year range (--from-year, --to-year)
Exports results in EndNote (.enw), RIS, and Zotero RDF formats
Generates an HTML browser for interactive review and selection of candidate citations
Supports Chinese-language academic writing workflows with bilingual segment handling
limitations: All candidates are tagged 'metadata-only' and must be verified against abstracts before citing
Does not retrieve full-text content or verify claims against source material
Citation matching relies on Crossref metadata which may not always reflect the most recent publications
Limited to the predefined journal scope (Nature/CNS/Cell families); cannot search arbitrary journals

use_cases.title: Adding citations to a journal manuscript draft
use_cases.description: A graduate student pastes their manuscript text and receives a ready-to-import EndNote file with verified Nature/CNS candidate references for each claim.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students writing theses or journal papers

use_cases.title: Building a reference list for a review article
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a review uses the skill to systematically find supporting citations from top-tier journals across multiple paragraphs of their draft.

use_cases.title: Chinese-language academic writing with CNS references
use_cases.description: A Chinese-speaking researcher writing in Chinese gets citations with bilingual segment handling while searching the same Nature/CNS journal scope.

prompt_templates.title: Basic citation request
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Nature and Science citations to the following paragraph: [paste your text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick citation lookup for a single paragraph

prompt_templates.title: Scoped search with year filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Find CNS citations for this manuscript section, restricted to papers published between 2020 and 2025: [paste text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted search with publication date constraints

prompt_templates.title: Chinese-language manuscript
prompt_templates.prompt: 帮我为以下论文段落添加Nature系列和CNS子刊的引用：[粘贴中文段落]

prompt_templates.title: Batch processing long article
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this 5000-word manuscript in batches and export all unique CNS citations as a single RIS file: [paste or reference file]
prompt_templates.scenario: Large manuscript with many segments requiring batched processing

output_examples.input: Find a Nature citation for: 'Recent advances in CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing have enabled precise modifications in mammalian cells.'
output_examples.output:
Suggested citation: (Doudna et al., 2014) - The new frontier of genome engineering with CRISPR-Cas9. *Nature* (2014). https://doi.org/10.1038/nature14566

Family: Nature
Support grade: metadata-only candidate - verify abstract before citing.

output_examples.input: Add CNS citations to this paragraph with papers from 2022-2024 only
output_examples.output:
Segment 1 → (Wang et al., 2023) Nature Medicine | (Chen et al., 2022) Cell
Segment 2 → (Lee et al., 2024) Nature Methods

Export file: references.enw (EndNote format)
Review browser: review.html

best_practices: Always verify candidate citations against the abstract or publisher page before including them in your manuscript
Use the --from-year and --to-year flags to restrict searches to relevant publication periods for your field
Start with a smaller scope (--scope flagship) before expanding to subjournals to reduce false positives
For long manuscripts, use --batch-size to process segments in chunks and avoid rate limiting from Crossref
anti_patterns: Do not cite a paper solely because its title is related to your claim without reading the abstract
Do not rely on this skill for journals outside the Nature/Science/Cell families - it will return no results
Do not use the output as a final reference list without manually verifying each citation's relevance and accuracy

faq.question: Which journals does this skill search?
faq.answer: Only flagship and subjournal titles from Nature Portfolio, the AAAS Science family, and Cell Press. It will not search general medical or field-specific journals outside these families.

faq.question: Does it verify that citations support my claims?
faq.answer: No. All candidates are marked 'metadata-only' and you must check the abstract or full text to confirm a paper actually supports your specific claim before citing it.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: EndNote (.enw) by default, with optional RIS and Zotero RDF export. You can also generate a review HTML browser for interactive candidate selection.

faq.question: How does it handle Chinese-language input?
faq.answer: The skill detects Chinese text and follows a dedicated Chinese-mode workflow that preserves Chinese notes while using English search queries against Crossref.

faq.question: Can it search for papers by DOI?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --doi or --doi-file flags to fetch and export specific papers by their DOI identifier directly from Crossref.

faq.question: Is an internet connection required?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill queries the Crossref metadata API in real time to find candidate citations. Offline operation is not supported.
